compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
**Completion verification result: PASS**

All three acceptance criteria are satisfied by the implementation in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/`.

**AC1 ‚Äî Sign + material-commit bind + invalidation-on-material-edit; coverage recorded.**
- HMAC-SHA256 signing: `attest.sign_plan_review()` (line 56‚Äì62) calls `signing.sign_manifest()`, which embeds `head_sha` (git-commit binding) and the HMAC under the environment key.
- Material fingerprint: `orchestrator.material_fingerprint()` (lines 701‚Äì712) hashes `ticket_id + description + file_impact + children` with SHA-256; explicitly excludes tags/comments/links/assignee.
- The fingerprint is bound into the signed manifest (`attest.build_manifest`, line 38: `f"material: {material}"`).
- Invalidation: `claim_gate_check()` (`attest.py` lines 106‚Äì123) recomputes the fingerprint at claim time and blocks with `stale-material` if it changed.
- Coverage: assembled in the orchestrator (`coverage` dict covering DET-floor per-check results, routing, LLM call counts, blocking/advisory counts); recorded in the schema (`plan_review_verdict.schema.json` line 46‚Äì50) and in the signed manifest (`blocking`/`advisory` lines in `build_manifest`).

**AC2 ‚Äî Externally CI-checkable (signed result + coverage).**
- `rebar review-plan <id>` CLI (`_cli/__init__.py` lines 290‚Äì335) exits 0 (PASS) / 1 (BLOCK) / 2 (INDETERMINATE) and emits JSON (with `--output json`) that includes both `signature` and `coverage` fields, making it scriptable as a CI step. Documented in `plan-review-gate.md` lines 31‚Äì32.

**AC3 ‚Äî Reuses the existing HMAC signing, no new key custody.**
- `attest.py` lines 3‚Äì10 document the reuse explicitly; `sign_plan_review()` calls `rebar.signing.sign_manifest()` verbatim ‚Äî the same function used by the close gate. No new key material, no new custody mechanism.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 REVIEW ‚Äî criterion T1 (Prior-art / novel-architecture justification) [overlay]

VERDICT: clean ‚Äî no findings.

A security/signing bright-line fires, so T1 applies. The plan satisfies it on all three sub-checks:

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria passed):
- T1(a) Prior art considered / not reinventing: The What/Scope explicitly reuse the existing close-gate signing machinery ("reuse the close-gate signing machinery"; "Reuse the close-gate signing; no new key custody"). This is grounded in the live surface ‚Äî `docs/reuse-surface.md` ¬ß1 ("HMAC-SHA256 attestation over a ticket's manifest of verified steps ‚Ä¶ reuse it for any 'this was verified' attestation. No new key custody is ever needed") and `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py` ("reuses the close-gate signing machinery verbatim ‚Ä¶ no new key custody"). The plan builds on existing prior art rather than reinventing it.
- T1(b) Novel-pattern justification: no novel architecture is introduced. HMAC-SHA256 is a standard primitive; the material-fingerprint binding is layered on the established head_sha freshness-binding pattern already in `rebar.signing` / `attest.build_manifest`. No anti-repackaging / Rule-of-Three concern.
- T1(c) Unverified capability assertions resolved: the load-bearing reuse claims ("reuses existing HMAC signing, no new key custody"; "integrity/forgery/replay/staleness model inherited") are verified against `docs/reuse-surface.md` and the implemented `attest.py` ‚Äî not unresolved assertions.

A well-specified plan that reuses an established, in-repo primitive is a PASS for T1.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 REVIEW (criterion G6 ‚Äî approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection)

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria this chunk passes):
- G6 FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: the approach (HMAC-sign the review result + invalidate on material edit + record coverage) directly produces the named "composable CI rigor / anti-vibe-coding signal" ‚Äî it solves the real problem, not a proxy. Confirmed implementable against the existing surface (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py`, `src/rebar/signing.py`).
- G6 APPROACH SELECTION: the chosen approach is defensibly at-least-as-good as the obvious structural alternative (bind invalidation to a git-commit SHA only). A code-commit binding alone would miss ticket-content edits that produce no commit; the plan's content-fingerprint-of-material approach is the more robust choice and aligns with what the codebase already does (`orchestrator.material_fingerprint`). No superior missed alternative ‚Äî discarded.
- G6 POSITIVE RATIONALE: present and adequate ‚Äî "reuse the close-gate signing machinery; no new key custody; integrity/forgery/replay/staleness model inherited" states why this approach fits. PASS (no rejected-alternatives section required).

FINDINGS (coaching, not blockers):

- [ ] Reconcile the stated binding mechanism with the actual two-part binding, so the implementer builds the invalidation that works. (criteria: G6 / mechanism correctness) ‚Äî location: "What" bullet, phrase "bind to the latest MATERIAL event commit". Evidence: the sound, in-code mechanism is NOT a "material event commit"; it is TWO separate bindings ‚Äî (1) a `head_sha` code-HEAD git binding (audit/freshness; `attest.claim_gate_check` lines 95‚Äì102) and (2) a SHA-256 **content fingerprint** of `{ticket_id, description, file_impact, children}` hashed into the signed manifest (`orchestrator.material_fingerprint` lines 723‚Äì734, bound via `attest.build_manifest` line 36). It is the content fingerprint ‚Äî not any "commit" ‚Äî that invalidates on material edits. The plan's "latest MATERIAL event commit" phrasing risks an implementer wiring invalidation to a commit SHA, which would NOT detect a ticket-only material edit (description/AC change with no new code commit). suggested_fix: reword to "bind a content fingerprint of the material fields (description/AC/file_impact/decomposition) into the signed manifest; additionally record code-HEAD (`head_sha`) for freshness ‚Äî invalidate the attestation when EITHER changes."

- [ ] State how "AC" and "decomposition" map onto the fingerprint basis, since the current basis hashes neither directly. (criteria: G6 / mechanism correctness, edge-case completeness) ‚Äî location: "What" bullet, "invalidate on material edits (description/AC/file_impact/decomposition‚Ä¶)". Evidence: `material_fingerprint` (orchestrator.py 727‚Äì732) hashes `description`, `file_impact`, and `children` (child IDs, sorted) ‚Äî it does NOT hash an `AC` field separately (AC lives inside `description` here) and it captures decomposition only as the SET of child ids, so an edit to a CHILD's own material content (re-decomposition of a child's body) would not flip the parent fingerprint. The plan asserts all four fields are covered; clarify whether that is exact (AC‚äÜdescription; decomposition = child-id set) so the AC's invalidation claim is verifiable and not over-promised. suggested_fix: (empty ‚Äî author should confirm intended granularity before wording.)

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 REVIEW ‚Äî criterion T3 (Integration feasibility) [overlay]

VERDICT: clean ‚Äî no findings (not-applicable / PASS).

T3 is an overlay that applies only when the plan integrates an EXTERNAL API/CLI/service/library or asserts a capability it has not used before. This plan does neither:

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria passed):
- T3(a) technical_feasibility: The integration is achievable as described and reuses an EXISTING in-repo primitive. "HMAC-sign the review result under the MCP environment key (reuse the close-gate signing machinery)" maps directly to `rebar.signing.sign_manifest()` / `compute_signature()` (src/rebar/signing.py:203, ALGORITHM = "HMAC-SHA256" at :50), already called verbatim by `attest.sign_plan_review()` (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py:56-62). No capability gap.
- T3(b) named-capabilities MATCH: the asserted reuse surface exists and matches ‚Äî material-commit/head_sha binding and material-edit invalidation are already implemented (`attest.claim_gate_check`, `orchestrator.material_fingerprint`), and the reuse contract is documented (docs/reuse-surface.md ¬ß1, docs/event-schema.md SIGNATURE row). This is an internal, already-used integration ‚Üí not a MISMATCH/UNVERIFIED external dependency.
- T3(c) auth/HTTPS preconditions: not-applicable ‚Äî no network/external service. The claim path is explicitly "no-llm/no-network ‚Ä¶ a pure local HMAC verify" (attest.py:73; orchestrator.py:580). Key custody is inherited, not new ("no new key custody").
- T3(d) SPIKE routing: not required ‚Äî there is no critical capability gap; the primitive is established in-repo.

Per T3's ANTI-FP rule ("an internal, already-used integration is not-applicable"), this chunk is a clean PASS for T3. (Note: mechanism-correctness nuances around the "MATERIAL event commit" phrasing vs. the actual content-fingerprint binding are real, but those are G6/mechanism findings already surfaced ‚Äî not a T3 external-integration feasibility gap.)

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: PASS-1 review (G6 ‚Äî approach soundness): PASS, no findings. The chosen mechanism (reuse signing.sign_manifest / the SIGNATURE event under the env HMAC key, bind a material fingerprint into the signed manifest, inherit code-HEAD freshness, exclude tags/comments/links/assignee from invalidation) matches the implemented design in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py and docs/plan-review-gate.md ¬ßAttestation; mechanism is correct and fit-for-purpose. Structural alternatives (detached-signature artifact / content-addressed verdict store) are clearly worse on key-custody, blast-radius, and codebase-alignment, so the chosen approach is defensible. Positive rationale is stated (Why: composable CI rigor; Scope: reuse close-gate signing, no new key custody, inherited integrity/forgery/replay/staleness model).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: PASS-1 review (G6 ‚Äî approach soundness): PASS, no findings. Mechanism verified against the implemented surface (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py, orchestrator.material_fingerprint, src/rebar/signing.py, docs/reuse-surface.md ¬ß1). The chosen approach ‚Äî reuse the close-gate HMAC-SHA256 signing, bind the material fingerprint INTO the signed manifest (so HMAC protects it), invalidate by recompute-and-compare plus inherited code-HEAD freshness ‚Äî is logic-complete, has no TOCTOU (the fingerprint is inside the signed payload), fails closed on an unrecoverable fingerprint, and converges LWW on concurrent signs. Material scope (description/AC/file_impact/decomposition; not tags/comments/links/assignee) matches material_fingerprint() exactly. Reviewer-generated alternatives (separate key custody; storing the fingerprint as an unsigned sibling field) are both strictly worse on blast-radius/robustness; the chosen approach is defensibly at-least-as-good on every axis. Positive rationale is stated (Why: composable CI rigor / anti-vibe-coding signal; Scope: no new key custody, integrity/forgery/replay/staleness model inherited).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Attestation (attest.py): build_manifest ('plan-review: PASS' + material fingerprint), sign_plan_review (reuses rebar.signing.sign_manifest -> SIGNATURE event), claim_gate_check (certified + code-HEAD freshness + material-edit invalidation; fail-CLOSED on unverifiable). material_fingerprint binds description/AC/file_impact/decomposition. Interface-tested. verify-completion: PASS.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
**Completion verification result: PASS**

All three acceptance criteria are satisfied by the implementation in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/`.

**AC1 ‚Äî Sign + material-commit bind + invalidation-on-material-edit; coverage recorded.**
- HMAC-SHA256 signing: `attest.sign_plan_review()` (attest.py:56‚Äì62) calls `rebar.signing.sign_manifest()`, which embeds `head_sha` (git-commit binding) and the HMAC under the environment key (signing.py:319‚Äì359).
- Material fingerprint: `orchestrator.material_fingerprint()` (orchestrator.py:723‚Äì734) hashes `ticket_id + description + file_impact + children (sorted)` with SHA-256; explicitly excludes tags/comments/links/assignee.
- Fingerprint bound into the signed manifest: `build_manifest()` (attest.py:36) inserts `f"material: {material}"` so the HMAC covers it.
- Invalidation: `claim_gate_check()` (attest.py:106‚Äì123) recomputes the fingerprint at claim time and blocks with `stale-material` if it changed; fails closed if fingerprint can't be recomputed.
- Coverage: assembled in the orchestrator (orchestrator.py:467‚Äì592) ‚Äî DET-floor per-check results, routing, LLM call counts, blocking/advisory counts ‚Äî and recorded in the schema (plan_review_verdict.schema.json:46‚Äì50) and the signed manifest (attest.py:39‚Äì40). MET.

**AC2 ‚Äî Externally CI-checkable (signed result + coverage).**
- `rebar review-plan <id>` CLI (_cli/__init__.py:290‚Äì335) exits 0 (PASS) / 1 (BLOCK) / 2 (INDETERMINATE) and emits JSON (default `--output json`) that includes both `signature` and `coverage` fields, making it scriptable as a CI step. MET.

**AC3 ‚Äî Reuses the existing HMAC signing, no new key custody.**
- attest.py:3‚Äì10 documents the reuse explicitly; `sign_plan_review()` calls `rebar.signing.sign_manifest()` verbatim (attest.py:62) ‚Äî the same function used by the close gate (transition.py:384). No new key material, no new custody mechanism. MET.

VERDICT: PASS

compiled_state.description:
## Why
The signed result is the composable CI rigor / anti-vibe-coding signal.

## What
- HMAC-sign the review result under the MCP environment key (reuse the close-gate signing machinery); bind to
  the latest MATERIAL event commit; invalidate on material edits (description/AC/file_impact/decomposition;
  not tags/comments/links/assignee); record coverage.

## Scope
- Reuse the close-gate signing; no new key custody. Integrity/forgery/replay/staleness model inherited.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sign + material-commit bind + invalidation-on-material-edit; coverage recorded.
- [ ] Externally CI-checkable (signed result + coverage).
- [ ] Reuses the existing HMAC signing, no new key custody.
compiled_state.title: Attestation: signing + material-commit binding + invalidation
